headline: Visa’s New AI Dispute Tools Target Fraud Costs And Support Growth Narrative
summary: Visa (NYSE:V) has introduced six new AI powered tools to overhaul how credit card disputes are handled. The tools focus on automating case analysis, generating merchant responses, and providing real time insights across the payments chain. Visa aims to reduce fraud related losses and inefficiencies tied to manual, fragmented dispute workflows for issuers, merchants, and payment processors. Visa, trading around $300.8, has recorded a 36.2% total return over the past 3 years and 41.4% over...

headline: Wirex and Ultra Stellar Launch Native Stellar Payment Infrastructure to Power Millions of Users and AI Agents
summary: Wirex, a leading stablecoin payment infrastructure provider serving over 7 million users globally, and Ultra Stellar, the company behind Stellar's largest wallet LOBSTR and leading decentralized exchange StellarX, today announced the launch of a native Stellar payment infrastructure, built directly on Soroban, Stellar's smart contract platform.

headline: Visa Defines the Next Era of Commerce: When AI Becomes the Customer
summary: SAN FRANCISCO, April 02, 2026--A majority of business leaders are preparing for a world where artificial intelligence doesn’t just assist commerce, it participates in it. New research from Visa (NYSE:V) underscores how quickly that shift is gaining momentum.

raw.headline: Visa Defines the Next Era of Commerce: When AI Becomes the Customer
raw.summary: SAN FRANCISCO, April 02, 2026--A majority of business leaders are preparing for a world where artificial intelligence doesn’t just assist commerce, it participates in it. New research from Visa (NYSE:V) underscores how quickly that shift is gaining momentum.

headline: Visa CMO Frank Cooper on the B2AI Study & Visa’s Approach to Commerce’s Next Era
summary: Visa CMO Frank Cooper joins Inside the ICE House to discuss the company’s B2AI study, which examines the rise of AI agents as a new customer segment and highlights trust, control, and security as the key barriers to AI‑driven commerce. Cooper explains how Visa is using the findings to build trusted infrastructure that enables AI transactions while keeping humans at the center. The conversation also explores Visa’s evolving approach to sports and events marketing, from long‑standing global partnerships like the Olympics and World Cup to a broader shift from traditional sponsorship to fan‑focused, creator‑led experiences.
